title: Some clarification on the structure of NERV HQ
display_title: Some clarification on the structure of NERV HQ
wikitext:
[]: Some clarification on the structure of NERV HQ             Quote Post by Dimorphic » Sat Oct 20, 2012 7:59 am Now, I'm about to ask a question that will probably sound stupid to you Evangelion veterans, but I'm asking it anyway because it's seriously bugging me and I can't get myself to finish the TV series without at least understanding some basic concept here. So there is this Geo-Front where NERV holds its operations and where buildings can reside during emergencies. Up till Episode 11 I thought that I had a basic understanding on how this all plays out more or less, but after watching that episode I'm completely confused about multiple things. - Firstly, it made me realize that I actually have no idea how these Eva Units get launched to the surface. The HQ, where this all takes place, is supposed to be this establishment, right? So when they launch these EVAs, where do these sudden launching shafts come from? Where are these so-called 'Eva pens' where they are kept to begin with? When having an overview of the Geo-Front, there's a whole lot of nothingness between the HQ and the actual surface, right? - Secondly, about episode 11 itself. The characters have to get from the surface to the HQ on foot, by going through vents and small corridors. Again, how do they get from the surface, to eventually falling through a vent and making it inside HQ when there is so much space in between? They are never shown going down, only horizontal. They even accidentally make it up to the surface again at some point, so they are actually right below the surface during that time. It might be quite laughable for me to be this confused about something seemingly so obvious that the series doesn't even feel the need to properly explain how this works. Nobody else seems to have a problem with it, so I guess it's my personal problem here. Any clarification would be greatly appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sat Oct 20, 2012 10:00 am This is one of those things in Eva you just not think about too much or ideally fanwank something. http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Supplemental_(Elevators_and_Eva_Lifts)

[]:             Quote Post by Dimorphic » Sat Oct 20, 2012 12:31 pm Fireball wrote: This is one of this things in Eva you just not think about too much or ideally fanwank something. http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Supplemental_(Elevators_and_Eva_Lifts)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 1:22 am Still not totally sure how they handle Eva launches, but there is a system of suspended railways to deal with the human traffic. In the very first episode, Misato and Shinji come into the Geofront on a suspended car train. You can see them in this badly-compressed screenshot: The tall building next to the pyramid is called the Subterminal, according to production drawings.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Oct 26, 2012 7:06 am If the Geo Front works the same in NME as in ETV, then I think that the puplic transportation railways as Reichu showed as function as EVA railways too. I think that, because in NME, while Misato ascends to the Geo Front in one of those railways, EVA02 with Mari onboard rushes to the surface in the same hatch. I hope that was understandable.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 26, 2012 1:38 pm The logistical problem is that the city is over the geofront void; but the EVA exit points are in the city (except when they exit into the geofront void from below) -- so either the cages are in the geofront ceiling (except for those times where they aren't), or there are portals on the way that spare us the sight of ballistic EVA units hurtling through the void.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 2:04 pm Looking at the Geofront dimensional diagram, and knowing that the Eva cages are located somewhere below the Geofront surface level: I don't think it would be difficult for Eva surface launch chutes to completely bypass the open part of the Geofront.
summary: []: Some clarification on the structure of NERV HQ             Quote Post by Dimorphic » Sat Oct 20, 2012 7:59 am Now, I'm about to ask a question that will probably sound stupid to you Evangelion veterans, but I'm asking it anyway because it's seriously bugging me and I can't get myself to finish the TV series without at least understanding some basic concept here. So there is this Geo-Front where NERV holds its operations
